commands.repair_reason: Contributor failure-path proof and maintainer/security review of the explicit URL credential contract are needed before merge; do not queue an automated repair while the proof gate is still unmet.; Needs attention: The patch still allows explicit gateway URLs to receive implicit local credentials in two status paths.; Review finding: [P1] Keep explicit gateway-status URLs off local auth — src/commands/gateway-status... (sha=f18cb65f755572e3c1c66d9d13314454c683f648)
commands.review_summary:
The PR threads a gateway probe auth `failureReason` through status and gateway-status paths so unresolved local credentials can fail before a WebSocket probe.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection of the latest diff shows explicit URL targets still resolve local auth, daemon RPC URL overrides still receive resolved daemon auth, and raw unset auth mode still bypasses fail-fast before the effective configured auth surface is considered.
commands.review_followup: Contributor failure-path proof and maintainer/security review of the explicit URL credential contract are needed before merge; do not queue an automated repair while the proof gate is still unmet.

target.title: fix(gateway): fail fast on missing local probe auth
target.body:
## Summary
- fail fast for local loopback probe/status auth resolution when gateway auth is required but unresolved
- return a local probe failure instead of attempting an anonymous loopback websocket connect
- add focused regression coverage for the local authenticated probe/status paths

## Testing
- pnpm vitest run src/gateway/probe-auth.test.ts src/commands/gateway-status/helpers.test.ts src/commands/status.scan.shared.test.ts

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Before this PR, when a gateway target's `gateway.auth.token` was a SecretRef that couldn't resolve (env var missing/renamed, vault path broken), `openclaw gateway probe` would still attempt to connect with empty auth — burning a few seconds on a doomed WebSocket handshake and returning a misleading "connect failed" instead of a specific "auth token unresolvable" diagnostic. After this PR, the probe-auth resolver surfaces the SecretRef diagnostic immediately and the probe runner skips the connect for `localLoopback` and (since the follow-up commit) `explicit` URL targets.
- **Real environment tested**: `mac-mini.lan` production host running `upgrade-2026.5.12` (which carries this commit). Gateway is admin-capable via SecretRef-backed auth, so the resolver path is exercised on every probe.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  ssh alexm@mac-mini.lan 'openclaw gateway probe'
  ```
- **Evidence after fix (live terminal output from mac-mini.lan, 2026-05-18)**:
  ```
  Reachable: yes
  Capability: admin-capable
    Connect: ok (51ms) · Capability: admin-capable · Read probe: ok
  ```
  This is the happy-path proof: the resolver successfully resolves `gateway.auth.token` from its SecretRef, the probe completes in 51 ms with `Capability: admin-capable`, and the gateway runtime accepts the resolved credential. The same machinery is what catches missing/unresolvable SecretRefs and short-circuits — the unit tests `helpers.test.ts > redacts resolver internals from unresolved SecretRef diagnostics` and `daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts > degrades safely when daemon probe auth SecretRef is unresolved` lock in the failure path that production doesn't exercise today.
- **Observed result after fix**: On a host where SecretRefs DO resolve, the probe completes cleanly with capability detection (admin/write/read) in under 100 ms. On a host where they don't (the failure path), the resolver returns `failureReason` carrying the joined diagnostics ("gateway.auth.token SecretRef is unresolved (env:default:NAME).") and the probe runner skips the WebSocket connect for both `localLoopback` and `explicit` URL kinds rather than blocking on a doomed handshake.
- **What was not tested**: Live SecretRef fault-injection on production (would require deliberately breaking auth on a host I rely on). The failure-path behavior is covered by the new test cases added in this PR — exercised on every CI run on every commit.
target.author: amittell

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
